title: Wireless Design, Configuration, and Validation · Independent Network Trial
summary: Design staff, guest, and point-to-point wireless service across crowded, regulated, and mobile spaces. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a secure wireless survey and deployment plan with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare relevant 802.11 capabilities across 2.4, 5, and 6 GHz bands, channel availability, range, and client support.
Choose channel, width, reuse, and band steering from regulatory domain, overlap, contention, capacity, and coverage.
Distinguish SSID, BSSID, ESS, hidden names, and client discovery without treating an SSID as a security control.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Perform predictive and measured surveys and verify RSSI, SNR, interference, utilization, roaming, and application outcomes.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Net Wireless Survey Roaming condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Wireless Survey Roaming applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Perform predictive and measured surveys and verify RSSI, SNR, interference, utilization, roaming, and application outcomes.

hints: Name the Net Wireless Survey Roaming precondition or invariant that the lab case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed lab case derived from this module: network wireless. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Strong RSSI alone proves a wireless cell has sufficient capacity and quality.
